evals.prompt: I have a 15-minute conference talk next month on our machine-learning results. Can you build me the slide deck?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-slides: plans a ~15-18 slide deck following the story arc (hook, context, gap, approach, results, implications, closure), allocates 40-50% of time to results, generates slides with generate_slide_image.py (attaching the previous slide for formatting consistency) and combines them via slides_to_pdf.py, applying visual-first, minimal-text design.

assertions.value: Plans a talk-appropriate slide count and narrative arc for the stated duration and uses the Nano Banana Pro generate/combine workflow (generate_slide_image.py + slides_to_pdf.py) with visual-first, minimal-text slides.

evals.prompt: I'm defending my dissertation in two weeks and need a 60-minute defense presentation covering all my studies.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-slides in defense mode: structures a comprehensive 45-60 minute deck covering all studies, prepares for examiner questions, and can use the LaTeX Beamer defense template (beamer_template_defense.tex) or the PDF/Nano Banana Pro workflow, with timing/pacing guidance.

assertions.value: Tailors structure and timing to a thesis-defense context (comprehensive coverage of all studies, Q&A prep) rather than a short conference format.

evals.prompt: Make me a LaTeX Beamer deck for a seminar talk that's heavy on equations and algorithm pseudocode.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-slides via the LaTeX Beamer path: selects the seminar template (beamer_template_seminar.tex), writes native LaTeX for equations and algorithms, compiles to PDF, and converts to images for visual validation.

assertions.value: Chooses the LaTeX Beamer implementation for math/algorithm-heavy content and uses the seminar template plus compile-and-validate workflow.

evals.prompt: Here's my finished talk PDF — check each slide for text overflow, overlapping elements, and fonts that are too small before I present.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scientific-slides visual-review workflow: converts the PDF to images with pdf_to_images.py, inspects each slide for overflow/overlap/small-font/contrast issues, and runs validate_presentation.py for slide count and font-size checks, producing an issue log.

assertions.value: Runs the slide validation/visual-review pass (pdf_to_images.py + validate_presentation.py) to flag overflow, overlap, and small fonts rather than re-authoring the deck.

evals.prompt: I need a PowerPoint poster for the conference poster session.
evals.expected_output: Defers to alterlab-pptx-posters and does NOT invoke alterlab-scientific-slides: a poster (single large-format page) is not a slide deck. scientific-slides builds multi-slide presentations for oral talks, not posters.

evals.prompt: Turn my talk's key findings into the Results section of my journal manuscript, written as full prose paragraphs.
evals.expected_output: Defers to alterlab-scientific-writing and does NOT invoke alterlab-scientific-slides: writing a manuscript's Results section in flowing prose is a paper-writing task, not building a presentation deck.
